hypothesis: Adam appears to be the better optimizer for topk at K=128 EF=12, and the 8e-4 callback landed very close to the 1e-3 anchor. A smaller follow-up LR reduction to 9e-4 can answer whether the adam optimum lies between those two settings rather than at either endpoint.
summary: Keep the topk mainline fixed at K=128 EF=12 with adam and run one more narrow LR refinement at 9e-4 to bracket the best adam operating point.
self_review: This keeps the family, K, EF, and optimizer fixed and asks one remaining LR question before moving on to other axes. The 8e-4 result was close enough to the 1e-3 anchor that an in-between LR is the most interpretable next probe.
notes_to_memory: Round 6 narrows the adam LR search between 8e-4 and 1e-3 for the current topk K=128 EF=12 mainline.
next_hypotheses: If 9e-4 does not clearly beat the existing adam anchor, stop LR tuning and move to a single loss/preprocess-related parameter next.
If 9e-4 improves again, keep adam as mainline and only then consider whether another very small LR refinement is still justified.
